channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Neudesic seeks to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI just updated its 187-page rulebook so ChatGPT can engage with more controversial topics
item.description: OpenAI has loosened the content rules for ChatGPT and its other AI models so they can "embrace intellectual freedom".
item.dc:creator: erichs211@gmail.com (Eric Hal Schwartz), TechRadar

item.title: Gen Z isn’t worried about China having access to their data. They should be
item.description: With TikTok and DeepSeek, young people are forking over sensitive personal data to the Chinese government. We should be worried. Among Gen Z, there’s a certain nihilism about China’s access to American data. Some argue that they have nothing to hide.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: Mexico says it’s awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New patch for Windows 11 24H2 reportedly plays havoc with File Explorer, and some folks are claiming it's broken their PC
item.description: Is Windows 11’s latest patch one to avoid for now? Reports suggest it’s causing serious problems with some PCs, but if you hold off, you’ll be left without security fixes.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The only way is stack! Arm wants to build its own CPU server for hyperscalers, reports say, and AMD won't be happy
item.description: A report says Arm is trying to recruit talent from some of its largest customers to help develop its own chips.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Cultura y tecnología, palancas para accionar la transformación digital del sector público
item.description: Para que el sector gubernamental sea verdaderamente productivo, es fundamental priorizar tanto la cultura como la tecnología. Así lo destaca un nuevo informe de The Economist Impact respaldado por SAS.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los CIO y los CISO se enfrentan a DORA: desafíos clave de cumplimiento
item.description: En vigor desde enero, la Ley de Resiliencia Operativa Digital (DORA) ha exigido un esfuerzo considerable a los directores de sistemas de la información (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) y a los directores de seguridad de la información (CISO) de 20 tipos de entidades financieras para lograr su cumplim
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Marvel Rivals director gives hope to the idea of a Nintendo Switch 2 port
item.description: Marvel Rivals could come to Nintendo Switch 2 in lieu of the original hardware, according to its producer.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GenAI can make us dumber
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) tools based on deep learning are quickly gaining adoption, but their use is raising concerns about how they affect human thought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 and Z Flip 7 could have better battery life – but not for the reason you might expect
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 and Z Flip 7 reportedly have highly efficient screens, among other upgrades.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: A partnership between Jigsaw and this Kentucky city could be the future of civic engagement
item.description: Bowling Green, Kentucky, is known for being the city from which Corvettes roll off the production lines, and for Fruit of the Loom underwear, which is headquartered there.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: This DARPA-backed startup banked $100 million for its energy-slashing analog chips
item.description: A young DARPA-backed startup with a fresh spin on a low-power computer chip has raised over $100 million in a Series B funding round, a sign of the wild appetite for more energy-efficient ways to build bigger and better AI.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Just 32 percent in US trust AI: Study
item.description: Only about a third of Americans trust artificial intelligence (AI), according to new data from the Edelman Trust Barometer. Just 32 percent of people in the U.S. said they trust the rapidly developing technology, according to the annual global survey’s “Insights for the Technology Sector.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Local history buffs are turning to Facebook to share memories of bygone eras
item.description: Social media has a reputation for capturing ephemeral thoughts and images, but around the world, people are using Facebook for a different purpose, setting up groups to record and share images and memories of the past.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: For February’s Patch Tuesday, Microsoft rolls out 63 updates
item.description: Microsoft released 63 patches for Windows, Microsoft Office, and developer platforms in this week’s Patch Tuesday update. The February release was a relatively light update, but it comes with significant testing requirements for networking and remote desktop environments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nvidia forges healthcare partnerships to advance AI-driven genomics, drug discovery and more
item.description: Healthcare has the most significant upside, particularly in patient care, among all the industries applicable to AI. The healthcare industry creates massive amounts of data, but much of it lives in silos.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple’s emotional lamp and the future of robots
item.description: Pixar Animation Studios has an unusual logo. The basic logo is the word “Pixar.” But sometimes, an animated lamp named Luxo Jr. hops into the frame and jumps on the letter “i.” The lap exudes personality and represents Pixar’s ability to turn any object into a compelling character.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mother of Little Rock homicide victim said son was killed while trying to sell shoes on Facebook Marketplace
item.description: During the last week of January, four people were killed in Little Rock, including a 19-year-old selling shoes on Facebook Marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Gary Burton Jr., NewsNation

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S. to abide by a law that required TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the platform or face a Jan. 19 ban.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: AI-powered fitness revolutionizing workouts with adaptive equipment
item.description: “I’m a tech nerd really at heart, and it’s cool to be on that cutting edge,” said Trevor Gere, co-owner of The Smart Fit Method in Northwest Suburban Kildeer.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, NewsNation

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
